title: Impermanence hole: ~/.claude (15.9GB live agent state) survives reboots only by manual rescue — persist it properly
description:
Parent: sinnix-tco. Audit 2026-07-06 of the impermanence setup + pre-wipe backups.

## How the wipe/backup lifecycle actually works (verified, sound)
1. Every boot, initrd rollback-root (hosts/sinnix-prime/storage.nix:587-647) snapshots @ -> .snapshots/root.TIMESTAMP, purges @/home/*/.cache + @/var/cache + stale mountpoint payloads first, then deletes+recreates @ (incl. nested subvols).
2. borgbackup-root-snapshots (modules/backup.nix:914-1010, OnBootSec=45min + daily) archives each root.* snapshot to /outer-realm/backup/borg-root-snapshots-v1 (zstd, excludes: nix, home/*/.cache, root/.cache, var/cache, tmp, run, proc, sys, dev, mnt, persist, realm, outer-realms, swap) THEN DELETES the snapshot. Verified archives: root-root.20260611T154123, 20260629T103806, 20260629T110613, 20260630T203812 (= the 2026-06-30 22:38 CEST boot, UTC-stamped). So pre-wipe roots ARE durably archived; zero root.* snapshots on disk is NORMAL operation, not pruning drift.

## The hole
/home/sinity/.claude is NOT persisted (only .claude.json, .config/claude, .local/state/claude-code are; verified via /proc/self/mountinfo — no bind for the dir). Yet it holds 15.9GB of projects/ (session JSONLs incl. resume state + the per-project auto-memory dirs), plus .credentials.json (OAuth, would force re-login), history.jsonl, scratch/ (9MB, global cross-project notes per operational docs), file-history/, todos, agents/, commands/ (some entries are HM symlinks — CLAUDE.md is; commands/ and others are real dirs; enumerate at execution).
Evidence it was manually rescued after the 2026-06-30 wipe: current @ created 2026-06-30 22:38:16, but ~/.claude contains files with mtimes back to 2025-07 — someone cp -a restored it post-boot (also explains the stray bare '@' subvol sitting in /.snapshots: manual restore-night artifact; clean it up as part of this issue).
Without action, the NEXT reboot (pending for the NVIDIA fix!) wipes it again, recoverable only by another manual borg/snapshot restore with a day-latency archive.

## Fix
Persist ~/.claude — but NOT onto @persist on the MX500 (drive at ~104% rated NAND wear; projects/ is write-heavy JSONL churn). Follow the existing polylogue precedent: /home/sinity/.local/share/polylogue is realm-backed (bind from nvme0n1p3 into home). Do the same: move ~/.claude content to a /realm-backed location (e.g. @realm subvol path realm/state/claude) and bind-mount into /home/sinity/.claude via the persistence module (modules/persistence.nix owns the bind wiring; check sinnix.persistence.initrdScaffold needs a placeholder for early-boot). Codex (~/.codex) and Gemini (~/.gemini) are already persist-bound — this closes the asymmetry (Claude was the only agent CLI with unpersisted state).
Optional same-pass: prune projects/ (15.9GB; polylogue archives sessions durably — check polylogue coverage first via archive_coverage, then apply a retention window to raw JSONLs, e.g. keep 30d live).

## Also audited, NO action needed (do not re-litigate)
- /var/lib: all >5MB entries are persist-bound or subvol-mounted (sinex, transmission, tailscale, systemd, bluetooth, NM, open-webui, nixos, journal).
- ~/.local/share/sinnix-browser (14.7GB): ephemeral BY DESIGN (re-seeded from live Chrome profile each boot).
- ~/.cache: own @persist subvol (survives), while excluded from root archives (correct: cache).
- XDG dirs: configured to /realm/inbox paths; boot-created scaffolds in home are empty.
- polylogue home state: realm-backed mount (my persist-grep initially missed it — it binds from @realm not @persist; audit scripts must match both).

## Timing constraint
This SHOULD land in the pre-reboot switch batch (with sinnix-fjq/jr8/0zi per the sinnix-tco sequencing note) — or at minimum, before rebooting, take a manual copy: rsync -aHAX /home/sinity/.claude/ /realm/tmp/claude-state-prereboot/. The reboot must not depend on remembering a manual restore again.

## Verification
- findmnt /home/sinity/.claude shows the realm-backed bind after switch.
- Post-reboot (the pending NVIDIA one): ~/.claude intact, claude CLI resumes sessions, no re-login prompt.
- Stray /.snapshots/@ subvol removed; only root.* lifecycle artifacts appear there going forward.
notes:
2026-07-06 (session resume): confirmed still code-complete from prior session — storage.nix bind mount config + rsync mirror to /realm/state/claude both present in working tree, not yet switch-verified. One item still needs an explicit operator decision, not mine to make: a stray btrfs subvol (id 4154, path '@', found during the original investigation) is still sitting unmounted on the root filesystem. Prior session inspected it read-only and wanted to delete it based on its own unverified 'borg covers this' assertion but was correctly blocked by the auto-mode classifier (irreversible deletion, no explicit user instruction naming this resource). Left untouched. Flagging for operator: mount -o subvolid=4154,ro <dev> /mnt/check to inspect, or explicitly instruct deletion if you're confident it's the known 06-30 manual-restore artifact.
2026-07-06 (CORRECTION — misdiagnosis found): the whole premise of this bead was WRONG. ~/.claude has been a symlink to ~/.config/claude since 2026-04-03 (git -S claudeSymlink confirms: modules/features/dev/agent-tools.nix's home.activation.claudeSymlink = 'ln -sfn .config/claude $HOME/.claude', 3+ months old), and .config/claude is ALREADY persisted via its own separate bind mount (confirmed in this file, unrelated to this bead). The original investigation's 'du -sh ~/.claude shows 15.9GB unpersisted' finding was a misread: du transparently follows the symlink and reports .config/claude's already-persisted size. Verified directly: /home/sinity/.config/claude/projects is 16G, exactly matching /realm/state/claude/projects (the rsync mirror taken earlier this session from what was believed to be an independent ~/.claude) — confirming no separate unpersisted data ever existed; the mirror just duplicated already-safe data. REVERTED the entire storage.nix bind-mount addition (claudeStateRoot/claudeHomeMount, the new mounts-list entry, tmpfiles rules, initrdScaffold entry, the before= ordering) — it actively conflicted with the correct long-standing symlink design: had it succeeded, mounting over ~/.claude would have shadowed the symlink and redirected live Claude Code session writes away from .config/claude to a stale mirror mid-session. Incident during discovery: the mount attempt DID partially run during today's switch (home-manager's claudeSymlink activation raced ahead of the failed realm-scaffold.service and recreated the symlink cleanly, so no harm occurred, but it revealed the conflict). /realm/state/claude (20G rsync copy) is inert leftover, harmless, left in place — not cleaned up, operator's call. sinnix-6x3 should be treated as CLOSED/NOT-A-BUG: ~/.claude was never actually at risk. No further action needed once the revert switch lands.
close_reason: MISDIAGNOSIS — closing as not-a-bug. The original investigation concluded ~/.claude was an unpersisted 15.9GB directory and needed a realm-backed bind mount. Verified during this switch: ~/.claude has been a symlink to the already-persisted ~/.config/claude since 2026-04-03 (git -S claudeSymlink in modules/features/dev/agent-tools.nix). No independent unpersisted data ever existed — du/rsync against the symlink transparently reported .config/claude's already-safe size. The new bind-mount code actively conflicted with this design (would have shadowed live session writes with a stale mirror) and was reverted before landing. /realm/state/claude (20G rsync mirror) is harmless inert leftover, left in place. Parent epic sinnix-tco remains open (steps 2-5 still pending) — --force used since bd's dependency direction blocks this child close on the still-open parent, which is expected.

title: MX500 recovery: free 100-200G, restore btrfs unallocated headroom, reduce standing writes (drive at ~104% rated NAND wear)
description:
Parent: sinnix-tco. URGENT tier raised 2026-07-06: SMART attr 202 raw=104 on the MX500 (CT1000MX500SSD1, /dev/sdc) — with the known 1.9x internal write amplification (memory: reference_ssd_storage_characterization) the NAND is at ~104% of rated endurance (213TB host writes x1.9 ≈ 405TB vs 360TBW rating). The drive still works but every avoided write extends remaining life; plan for eventual replacement (separate decision). Filesystem: 931G, Data used 828GiB, **unallocated only 14.97GiB** — btrfs chunk-allocation starvation territory (ENOSPC-with-free-space risk + degraded allocator latency on the same disk the desktop's refault storm re-reads from).

## Measured layout (2026-07-06)
/nix 261G (weekly GC IS on, --delete-generations +10, keep-outputs=true keeps devshell dep closures via direnv gcroots) | @persist 279G real | @sinex 266G (blob-repository 167G + postgresql 99G) at /var/lib/sinex | /var/cache/sinex 118G = 3 per-checkout dev caches (51d4cbf4bc80, 566e9bf8d5e8=LIVE (running sinexd), a853b8a0179a) | journal 8.6G | .prewipe-snapshot-20260627: only 2.14GiB exclusive (NOT a big win) | 7 rolling half-hourly @persist snapshots: fine.

## Steps (order matters)
1. Stale sinex dev caches: confirm live checkout hash (ps aux | grep sinexd shows /var/cache/sinex/sinity/<hash>/; also derive from current sinex checkout per the devshell relocation logic in sinex flake). Delete the two stale dirs. Expected: ~60-80G. THIS IS A DELETE — list dirs + sizes and confirm hash mapping in the issue comment before rm.
2. Nix store: run `clean` (nh clean all wrapper). Then measure gcroots cost: nix-store --gc --print-roots | grep -v /proc | wc -l; identify direnv gcroots of dead/stale project shells (~/.local/share/direnv or per-project .direnv/); prune stale ones. Report freed GB (keep-outputs makes estimates unreliable — measure, don't promise).
3. Delete .prewipe-snapshot-20260627 (btrfs subvolume delete /var/lib/sinex/.prewipe-snapshot-20260627) — only 2.14G but it pins the pre-wipe state and its deletion simplifies future @sinex migration. Confirm with operator context: it was a safety snapshot for a June-27 wipe operation that evidently succeeded.
4. Restore unallocated headroom: after 1-3, run filtered balance: btrfs balance start -dusage=50 / (background slice, e.g. sinnix-scope background). Target: unallocated >= 50GiB. Do NOT run full balance (pointless wear).
5. Journald cap 32G->4G (coordinated with sinnix-0zi item 6).
6. Wear reduction audit (standing writes to sdc): /var/cache/nix-build (nix build-dir, chattr +C) is on root — evaluate moving build-dir to /realm/cache/nix-build (NVMe, 400GB/day budget vs 60GB/day SLO); check git log for why it landed on root (2026-05-23 era moved build scratch AROUND — find the current rationale before moving). /tmp is plain disk on sdc — consider boot.tmp.useTmpfsSize=6G tmpfs (tradeoff: RAM pressure; with zram-only swap this is acceptable for small files; heavy work already belongs on /realm/tmp per policy). Decide and implement one way, document.

## Verification
df -h / (target <75%), btrfs filesystem usage / (unallocated >=50G), smartctl -A /dev/sdc recorded before/after as wear baseline, all services still healthy (sinexd, postgres) after cache deletion.

comments.author: Codex
comments.text:
Step 1 (sinex dev caches) re-measured 2026-07-06 — layout has changed since the bead's original snapshot:

Current /var/cache/sinex/sinity/ contents (only 2 dirs, not 3):
- 566e9bf8d5e8 = sha256(/realm/project/sinex)[:12] — 113G — LIVE. Confirmed via `ps aux`: sinexd main process (PID 4076586) and two scan-source-driver processes run from /var/cache/sinex/sinity/566e9bf8d5e8/target/debug/sinexd. NOT deleting.
- 5206d3963775 = sha256(/realm/tmp/worktrees/sinex-bundle-verify)[:12] — 11G — maps to an existing, still-registered git worktree (`git -C /realm/project/sinex worktree list` shows it at commit 0cd422a94, detached HEAD, 3 unmerged commits, e.g. "defer rebuilds for observability probes"). Not merged into sinex master yet, so this is active/incomplete work, not stale debt. NOT deleting.

The two dirs the bead named as stale (51d4cbf4bc80, a853b8a0179a) no longer exist at all -- already cleaned up between the bead's original measurement and now (prior session / GC / normal churn). Total cache footprint is now ~124G (113+11), consistent with the bead's ~118G total, just redistributed differently. Net: step 1 has nothing safe to delete right now -- both existing dirs map to real, in-use checkouts. No rm performed for this step.

title: Kill policy: PSI-scoped systemd-oomd on expendable slices, earlyoom demoted to -m3 floor
description:
OPERATOR-DECIDED 2026-07-06 (approved: demote earlyoom now, decide retire-vs-keep after one observation week). Parent: sinnix-tco; synthesis §3b. DEPENDS ON sinnix-mys (needs swap to exist so cgroup reclaim has a degree of freedom) and sinnix-fjq (kill events must be recorded to evaluate).

## Rationale recap
Zero global kernel OOMs ever recorded; earlyoom generated the outages (69 kills 2026-06-24, 186 on 06-30 incl. six same-day threshold rewrites, 115 on 07-04 incl. 79x kitten cascade killing 23-59MiB helpers that freed nothing). Free-memory thresholds cannot see thrash (314MiB-avail stall with empty swap, 2026-06-30). PSI measures the stall itself; killing at cgroup granularity takes the whole offending scope, fixing victim-choice degeneracy.

## Exact changes
modules/performance.nix (or runtime-defaults.nix where slices are defined — runtime-defaults.nix:237-301):
1. systemd-oomd is ALREADY enabled (performance.nix:301-305) but manages nothing (grep confirms zero ManagedOOM* properties anywhere). Add to these slices ONLY:
   - system nix-build.slice, user build.slice, user nix-build.slice, system+user background.slice:
     ManagedOOMMemoryPressure=kill
     ManagedOOMMemoryPressureLimit=50%
     ManagedOOMMemoryPressureDurationSec=30s  (NOT the 10%/5s defaults that killed Codex on 2026-05-07)
   - Explicitly NOT on: agent.slice, app.slice, session.slice, system-critical.slice, backup.slice (false-positive history; agents are recovery state).
   - Delete the performance.nix comment claiming "intentionally does not use PSI-triggered kills" — superseded.
2. earlyoom (performance.nix:267-299): freeMemThreshold 5 -> 3 (patched physical-MemTotal semantics, overlay stays), keep freeSwapThreshold=100 (memory-only trigger), KEEP the avoid regex, REMOVE the prefer regex entirely (victim steering was the arms race; at -m3 it fires only at true exhaustion where score-based choice is fine).
3. Remove duplicate MemoryHigh/MemoryMax on nix-daemon unit (build-policy.nix:142-143) — the slice (runtime-defaults.nix:248-249) is the single source. Slice caps stay 22G/28G.

## Verification
- systemctl show nix-build.slice -p ManagedOOMMemoryPressure shows kill; oomctl lists the managed cgroups.
- Synthetic test (safe): run a memory-hog in a scope under build.slice (sinnix-scope build -- stress-ng --vm 1 --vm-bytes 26G) and confirm oomd kills the scope, not the desktop, and a kill_event row lands (sinnix-fjq).
- One-week evaluation via sinnix-kx4: if oomd handles all incidents and earlyoom never fires, open a follow-up to retire earlyoom + its overlay patch entirely.

## Anti-goals
Do NOT re-add per-slice MemorySwapMax (tried 2026-06-18, reverted 06-21, made pressure brittle). Do NOT touch agent.slice caps (2026-06-18 lesson: shared caps froze all agents).

title: Memory posture change: swappiness 10 + zram-only 8G, remove cache-trim, single coherent commit
description:
OPERATOR-DECIDED 2026-07-06 (approved: zram return; signal moves to PSI/refaults, not swap gauges). Parent: sinnix-tco; synthesis §3a. DEPENDS ON sinnix-fjq + sinnix-kx4 (telemetry first + a few days baseline). One commit, no transition period, no deprecation theater — remove superseded machinery in the same change.

## Exact changes (all in /realm/project/sinnix)
modules/performance.nix:
- vm.swappiness = 0 -> 10  (MGLRU is fully enabled, /sys/kernel/mm/lru_gen/enabled=0x0007; file reclaim still dominant at 10, cold anon becomes evictable)
- vm.vfs_cache_pressure = 1000 -> 100 (the value the 2026-06-07 comment actually credits)
- KEEP: vm.page-cluster=0, vm.min_free_kbytes=1048576, vm.watermark_scale_factor=200, dirty_background_bytes=64M, dirty_bytes=256M, all inotify/hung-task settings, sinnix-iocost-init (load-bearing: all IOWeight depends on it), sinnix-cpu-power-limits.
- REMOVE ENTIRELY: sinnix-cache-trim service + timer (drop_caches every 2min manufactures refaults; kernel LRU reclaim replaces it).
- zramSwap: enable=true, algorithm=zstd, memoryMax = 8GiB (fixed size, not percent — deterministic), priority=100.
- REWRITE the whole memory-block comment (performance.nix:154-180): current prose contradicts committed values (credits swappiness=1+vfs=100, code says 0+1000) and blames swappiness=60 for 2026-06-07 when repo had 10 configured (live host ran kernel default 60 = config/live drift, see drift-sentinel issue). New comment: cite .agent/scratch/2026-07-06-memory-posture-synthesis.md, state the failure modes this posture must survive (disk-swap livelock 2026-06-03 = impossible by construction with zram-only; cache-hoard = bounded by swappiness 10 + MGLRU; burst-alloc starvation = min_free/watermark; residue = post-switch zram reset hook).

hosts/sinnix-prime/storage.nix:
- REMOVE the 4GiB /swap/swapfile, its prepare-swapfile unit, swap-swapfile.swap wiring, and the swap drain timer (added 2026-06-30). No disk swap anywhere.

Keep the existing post-switch zram-reset hook (sinnix-zram-reset, added 2026-06-12) — it answers the residue objection.

earlyoom (same commit or M2, executor's call on commit boundary): freeSwapThreshold=100 stays only until M2 rewires kill policy.

## Ordering / safety
- Test with test-vm first is OPTIONAL (sysctl+zram are low-risk, VM does not reproduce pressure anyway). switch directly per repo policy, then verify:
  swapon --show (only /dev/zram0, prio 100, 8G), sysctl vm.swappiness vm.vfs_cache_pressure, systemctl list-timers | grep cache-trim (gone), zramctl.
- A reboot must have happened before this lands (kernel gen 20260616 still running as of 2026-07-06 02:00; NVIDIA userspace/kernel mismatch breaks new GPU apps).

## Success metrics (via sinnix-kx4 product, 1-week observation)
- workingset_refault_file delta/hour drops >10x vs 2026-07-05/06 baseline.
- pswpout stays near-zero except under genuine pressure; no sustained pswpin/pswpout cycling (that would be the 2026-06-07 signature — if seen, drop swappiness to 5, not to 0).
- Desktop lag reports at 22-29GB anon cease.

## Rollback
Single revert commit restores current posture; zram module is stateless across reboot.
notes: SPEC CORRECTION (2026-07-06 verification): sinnix-zram-reset does NOT exist anymore — it was removed with zram in the 2026-06-29 flip (zero grep hits in modules/ and scripts/). The description's 'keep the existing post-switch zram-reset hook' is WRONG. Instead: RECREATE the residue hygiene as part of this issue — a post-switch hook (activation script or switch wrapper step) that runs 'swapoff /dev/zram0 && swapon /dev/zram0' equivalent via zramctl reset when zram residue exceeds ~512MB with MemAvailable comfortable (>8G), mirroring the intent of the deleted 2026-06-12 tool. Check git log -S 'zram-reset' for the original implementation to adapt (commit era b46ca8a..8675c7d).

title: Lynchpin: ingest new reclaim/kill telemetry, fix substrate promotion stall, add pressure-incident product
description:
Companion to sinnix-fjq (capture side). Parent context: sinnix-tco; synthesis §3c. Work happens in /realm/project/sinity-lynchpin (cross-repo — this bead tracks it from the sinnix side because the redesign is sequenced here).

## What to do
1. Schema/readers: extend lynchpin/sources/machine_schema.py + machine_models.py + machine.py iterators for the new metric_sample vmstat columns, cgroup memory.events counters, and the new kill_event table (typed reader + validation, triple-fallback pattern like existing tables).
2. FIX PROMOTION STALL (bug, do first): substrate promotion of memory tables silently stopped 2026-06-30 — machine_cgroup_memory_sample has 15,007 rows spanning 4.5h while live SQLite has 447k rows through 2026-07-05; machine_service_state stops 2026-06-30 06:53. machine_metric_sample/gpu/network promote fine, so the cgroup/service-state promote steps specifically stalled. Find why in lynchpin/ingest/machine_materialize.py + whatever drives promotion; add a freshness check to readiness so a silent stall becomes a visible readiness failure ('missing data is missing, never zero' — attribution spec line 227).
3. Pressure-incident interpretation product: standing analysis that detects PSI-spike windows (memory/io some avg10 above threshold for sustained duration) and annotates each with: refault-rate delta (new vmstat counters), top-N cgroup memory deltas (cgroup_memory_sample), kill events in window, active workloads. Output artifact machine_pressure_incidents.json following existing analysis-product conventions (lynchpin/analysis/machine/). Feed it into the evidence graph (lynchpin/graph/machine_analysis.py) and give attribution candidates real pressure-vs-quiet deltas (machine_observational_deltas.json currently reports zero such deltas).
4. Bridge machine incidents into narrative/anomaly layers: lynchpin/graph/narrative.py and lynchpin/analysis/anomaly_crossref.py currently have ZERO references to machine/pressure/psi/memory — pressure incidents should surface in daily narratives.
5. Refresh stale artifacts: most machine_* analysis products are dated 2026-06-29/07-01; re-run after the above so the current era is covered.

## Verification
- Readiness check fails if promotion lags live by >24h (prove by test or manual stall simulation).
- machine_pressure_incidents.json reproduces the 2026-07-06 00:20-00:24 incident (earlyoom kills during first switch attempt, PSI spike, cc1plus victims) from recorded data alone.

## Success criterion for the whole redesign (measured here)
After the posture change (M1/M2 issues): refault rate per hour collapses by >10x vs the 2026-07-05/06 baseline; kills/day -> ~0; PSI memory full avg300 stays <5% during builds. The baseline window MUST be captured before M1 lands.

title: Capture vmstat reclaim counters + memory.events + kill events in machine telemetry
description:
Part of the memory-posture redesign (parent: sinnix-tco). MUST land BEFORE the posture change so before/after is measurable. Full context: /realm/project/sinnix/.agent/scratch/2026-07-06-memory-posture-synthesis.md §3c.

## Why
The 2026-07-06 investigation showed the lag mechanism (file-cache refault storm: workingset_refault_file=967M, pgscan_file=21.8e9, pgscan_anon=0) is INVISIBLE to the telemetry stack: no /proc/vmstat counters are captured anywhere, and earlyoom/oomd/kernel-OOM kill events exist only as unstructured journal lines. Lynchpin's metric_sample (10s cadence, SQLite at /realm/data/captures/machine/telemetry.sqlite -> /realm/db/machine-telemetry/telemetry.sqlite) already carries full PSI + memory breakdown.

## What to do (sinnix capture side)
1. Locate the telemetry sampler (modules/services/machine-telemetry.nix and whatever script/daemon writes metric_sample rows). Extend the 10s sample with cumulative counters from /proc/vmstat:
   workingset_refault_file, workingset_refault_anon, workingset_activate_file, workingset_activate_anon, pgscan_kswapd, pgscan_direct, pgsteal_kswapd, pgsteal_direct, pswpin, pswpout, allocstall_normal, allocstall_movable, oom_kill.
   Store raw cumulative values (consumers compute deltas), same pattern as existing psi *_total_us columns. Per the 2026-05-15 roadmap decision: EXTEND metric_sample, do NOT add a separate table.
2. Extend cgroup_memory_sample with memory.events counters per sampled cgroup: high, max, oom, oom_kill (read /sys/fs/cgroup/<path>/memory.events).
3. New kill-event capture: a journal-follow unit (or extension of existing sampler) that parses and persists structured rows for: earlyoom SIGTERM/SIGKILL lines (unit earlyoom, includes victim comm/pid/rss/oom_score), systemd-oomd kills, kernel OOM (kernel: Out of memory / Killed process), memcg OOM (kernel oom-kill with memcg path). Table e.g. kill_event(ts, killer, victim_comm, victim_pid, victim_rss_mib, cgroup_path, oom_score, raw_line). Journald is Storage=persistent SystemMaxUse=32G so history since ~2026-06-17 can be backfilled once at first run.
4. Move below.service into the observability resource class (system-critical.slice, Nice=-5, IO best-effort/0) so the 5s microscope survives pressure windows — the 2026-05-08 seed documented below being throttled during the exact incidents it should record. Check modules/ for where below.service is defined; it currently runs default priority in system.slice.

## Verification
- After switch: sqlite3 query on telemetry.sqlite shows new columns populating each 10s tick; kill_event backfill contains the known 2026-06-30 storm (186 rows) and 2026-07-04 (115 rows, 79x kitten).
- systemctl show below.service -p Slice,Nice confirms observability placement.

## Notes for executor
- Schema change must be coordinated with lynchpin readers (lynchpin/sources/machine_schema.py validates columns) — see companion lynchpin issue.
- Do not sample /proc/vmstat more often than the existing tick; it is one file read, negligible cost.
notes:
SPEC PIN (2026-07-06 verification): the sampler implementation is /realm/project/sinnix/pkgs/machine-telemetry/collector.py (Python, wrapped via writeTextFile in modules/services/machine-telemetry.nix:27-34; sampling intervals are module options in the same file). Extend metric_sample there; schema versioning: the module/collector carry schema_version — bump it and coordinate with lynchpin machine_schema.py per the companion issue.
2026-07-06: code-complete. collector.py SCHEMA_VERSION 4->5. metric_sample +13 vmstat cumulative counters (workingset_refault/activate file+anon, pgscan/pgsteal kswapd+direct, pswpin/pswpout, allocstall normal+movable, oom_kill). cgroup_memory_sample +4 memory.events counters (high/max/oom/oom_kill, distinct from existing memory_high_bytes/max_bytes which are config limits not event counts). New kill_event table + scan_kill_events(): journalctl -g pre-filtered grep, cursor persisted via source_status table (source=machine.kill_event), backfill since 2026-06-17 on first run. Patterns calibrated against REAL host incidents (2026-06-30 storm, 2026-07-04 79x-kitten storm: earlyoom regex verified against 149 real matching log lines); kernel-oom/memcg-oom/systemd-oomd patterns best-effort (no real samples on this host yet) but raw_line always preserved. below.service + machine-telemetry.service ALREADY resourceClass=observability (verified live via systemctl show — bead's premise that below ran default-priority was stale/already fixed, no change needed there). Full round-trip tested: init_db + ensure_columns migration path + all 3 new/extended tables insert correctly in a scratch sqlite db. NOT YET switch-verified (needs Step-1 batch switch + live telemetry.sqlite check).
close_reason: All items landed and switch-verified: telemetry.sqlite metric_sample has 13 new populating vmstat columns, cgroup_memory_sample has 4 new populating memory_events columns, kill_event table backfilled 572 rows since 2026-06-17 (patterns calibrated against this host's real 2026-06-30/07-04 incidents). below.service and machine-telemetry.service were already resourceClass=observability (verified live via systemctl show) — no change needed there, bead's premise was stale. Commit fa8e217.

title: Investigate interactive lag + OOM ceiling at ~28-29GB anon on sinnix-prime
description:
EPIC: memory/perf/config redesign for sinnix-prime. Investigation complete 2026-07-06; all findings + full evidence in /realm/project/sinnix/.agent/scratch/2026-07-06-memory-posture-synthesis.md. Operator approved all assessments (zram return, earlyoom demote, @sinex migration, nixpkgs-ai manual cadence). This description is the AUTHORITATIVE RUNBOOK — the note trail below is historical.

## EXECUTION ORDER (config work; capability designs uha/nqz/e8e/cc3/ulc/o3e/6o2 are unordered follow-ons)

STEP 1 — one switch batching these (no interdependencies, all reboot-independent):
  - sinnix-fjq  P1 telemetry capture (collector.py vmstat+memory.events+kill events; below priority)
  - sinnix-6x3  P1 persist ~/.claude realm-backed (MUST land before any reboot; interim copy already at /realm/tmp/claude-state-prereboot 2026-07-06)
  - sinnix-jr8  P2 build defaults + cudaCapabilities 8.6 + nixpkgs-ai split + entrypoint unification
  - sinnix-0zi  P2 streamlining removals + journald 4G cap + /realm noatime
  - sinnix-u63  P2 fix items only (borg-drill root-cause+OnFailure surfacing, LLMNR off, tailscaled classify)

STEP 2 — reboot (fixes NVIDIA userspace/kernel mismatch, kitty preview EGL failures, nvidia-container CDI; verify all three after).

STEP 3 — disk, immediately after reboot (independent of telemetry):
  - sinnix-een  P1 MX500 recovery (stale sinex caches, GC, snapshot delete, balance -dusage=50, build-dir/tmp wear decisions)
  - then sinnix-6b4 P2 @sinex -> /realm migration (needs een headroom; schedule a capture-gap window)

STEP 4 — lynchpin side (parallel to step 3):
  - sinnix-kx4  P1 ingest new columns, FIX PROMOTION STALL, pressure-incident product, narrative bridge
  - then hold a few days: baseline window with new counters recording.

STEP 5 — posture, one commit, after baseline exists:
  - sinnix-mys  P1 swappiness 10 + zram-only 8G + remove cache-trim + vfs 100 + remove swapfile machinery + RECREATE zram residue hook (see correction note — old sinnix-zram-reset was deleted 06-29)
  - then sinnix-3gb P1 oomd PSI kills on build/background slices + earlyoom -m3 floor
  - then 1-week observation against sinnix-kx4 success metrics (refault/hr -10x, kills/day ~0, PSI mem full <5% during builds); decide earlyoom retire.

ANYTIME after step 1: sinnix-mxi (kitty leak/MCP sweep/postgres/sinexd handoff), sinnix-p1n (drift sentinel).

## Key facts a cold agent needs
- Repo policy: solo direct-master; commit+push after verified switch; use devshell 'switch' (never raw nh); no standalone nix eval probes as hygiene.
- Sampler code: pkgs/machine-telemetry/collector.py. Slice defs: flake/data/... via modules/runtime.nix -> runtime-defaults.nix (audit map in issues). Rollback/wipe: hosts/sinnix-prime/storage.nix:587. Borg root archive job: modules/backup.nix:914.
- Host: 32GB RAM, RTX 3080 (sm_86), MX500 root at ~104% rated NAND wear (be gentle: no full balance, no bulk rewrites), /realm NVMe healthy.
- MGLRU enabled (0x0007). systemd-oomd already enabled but managing nothing. earlyoom package is overlay-patched (physical MemTotal percent) — keep patch while earlyoom exists.
notes:
Correction: hogkill v2 issue id is sinnix-nqz (oracle v2 = sinnix-uha).
2026-07-06: STEP 1 COMPLETE — all 5 issues (sinnix-jr8, sinnix-fjq, sinnix-0zi, sinnix-u63, sinnix-6x3) closed, landed in commit fa8e217, switch-verified live (telemetry schema populating, kill_event backfilled, LLMNR off, journald 4G, transmission/slice cleanup unaffected, build max-jobs=1/cores=16 live). sinnix-6x3 closed as MISDIAGNOSIS not completion — ~/.claude was already persisted via a 3-month-old symlink to ~/.config/claude; no independent unpersisted data ever existed, and the proposed bind-mount was reverted before landing (would have conflicted with the existing design). Only remaining failed unit is nvidia-container-toolkit-cdi-generator.service (expected — needs Step 2's reboot). NEXT: Step 2 (reboot — fixes NVIDIA userspace/kernel mismatch, kitty preview EGL failures, nvidia-container CDI; verify all three after), then Step 3 (sinnix-een MX500 recovery, then sinnix-6b4 @sinex migration), Step 4 (sinnix-kx4 lynchpin ingest, parallel to step 3), Step 5 (sinnix-mys memory posture + sinnix-3gb oomd/earlyoom, after a baseline observation window).

title: hogkill v2: PSS-truthful, cgroup-first, telemetry-backed hog triage with freeze and trajectory evidence
description:
Parent: sinnix-tco. Target: /realm/project/sinnix/scripts/hogkill (interactive gum-based inspector, added 2026-06-30 during the kill-storm era). Solid skeleton (unit detection from cgroup, user/system manager awareness, dry-run, confirm gates — keep all). Redesign around the three lessons this repo paid for:

## Lessons -> defects
1. RSS lies (2026-07-06: kitty RSS 2.8GB vs 662MB PSS — 550MB shared driver/font pages). hogkill sorts and displays ps RSS; the operator triages on inflated numbers.
2. Process-kill can be futile (2026-07-04 storm: 79 kitten kills freeing nothing). hogkill is process-first; the reclaim-relevant object is the cgroup subtree (what oomd kills, sinnix-3gb).
3. Point-in-time view cannot distinguish leak from legit workload (the kitty verdict required a telemetry trajectory query). hogkill shows now-state only.

## Redesign
1. PSS-truthful rows: for the displayed top-N (default 30), read /proc/PID/smaps_rollup (root may be needed for other users' processes; sudo -n fallback with graceful degrade to RSS + a '(rss)' marker). Columns: PSS, PSS_ANON (the honest number), SWAP, RSS. Default sort: pss_anon. Keep --sort cpu|mem for the CPU case.
2. Cgroup-first mode (new default top section): aggregate by unit/scope using cgroup memory.current + memory.stat anon (one read per cgroup, cheaper than per-PID smaps) — table of top units with anon/file/swap split and 'reclaim if killed' = anon estimate. The per-process table remains below (--processes for old behavior). This mirrors the oomd kill boundary so triage matches what the killer would actually do.
3. Trajectory evidence on selection: when a process/unit is selected, query /realm/data/captures/machine/telemetry.sqlite process_memory_sample (pid + process_start_time_ticks to survive pid reuse; unit-level via cgroup_memory_sample) for min/max/current pss_anon over the last 72h -> print 'grew 45MB -> 2.9GB over 3d (~1GB/day) — leak signature' vs 'stable around 1.1GB — steady-state workload'. Graceful skip if sqlite/table absent. This is the query that convicted kitty — one keystroke instead of hand-written SQL.
4. New actions: 'unit: freeze'/'unit: thaw' (systemctl freeze — reversible containment for I-need-RAM-now-but-do-not-want-to-lose-work; the 05-23 freezer incident note: warn that frozen scopes must be thawed, list currently-frozen units at startup). 'pid: pstree kill' option killing the selected process subtree.
5. Guardrails: highlight rows matching the earlyoom avoid regex (parse the live earlyoom cmdline) and agent sessions (claude/codex/kitty hosting them) with a WARNING tag — recovery-state processes, the 2026-06-15 lesson. Confirm text for those requires typing the unit name, not just y/n.
6. --json mode: top-K reclaim candidates with all evidence fields (for machine-explain playbooks and future automation; machine-explain sinnix-6o2 references hogkill as its kill-storm playbook action).

## Non-goals
No daemon/automation — hogkill stays an interactive/explicitly-invoked tool; automated killing belongs to oomd (sinnix-3gb). No PSI charts — machine-explain owns interpretation; hogkill shows the one-line PSI summary it already has.

## Verification
- Table check vs manual smaps_rollup for 3 processes (values match, kitty-class shared-page inflation visibly corrected vs RSS column).
- Trajectory: current fattest kitty must reproduce the known growth verdict from telemetry.
- Freeze/thaw round-trip on a scratch scope (systemd-run --user --scope sleep) leaves no frozen residue; frozen-units warning fires while frozen.
- Avoid-list highlight: verify a claude process row carries the WARNING tag.

title: sinnix preflight: reboot/switch safety gate (pending-change diff, mismatch prediction, unpersisted-valuables, live-work inventory)
description:
Parent: sinnix-tco. Motivation — every element observed live on 2026-07-06: (a) NVIDIA userspace/kernel mismatch after switches-without-reboot broke every new GPU app launch (kitty preview popups died at EGL init; nvidia-smi NVML mismatch; nvidia-container-toolkit-cdi-generator failed) and nobody knew until symptoms; (b) ~/.claude held 15.9GB of unpersisted state that a reboot would have destroyed (survived the 06-30 wipe only via manual rescue); (c) the 00:20 switch attempt ran during memory pressure and earlyoom shot its compilers; (d) booted generation (06-16) vs deployed (07-05) confusion cost investigation time.

## Design
`sinnix-preflight [reboot|switch]` — a read-only gate command (shipped like other sinnix-* helpers from the scripts/ + modules wiring) printing PASS/WARN/BLOCK per check, exit code reflects worst. Checks:

REBOOT mode:
1. Unpersisted-valuables scan: walk /home/sinity + /var/lib top levels; flag non-symlink, non-mount entries >100MB or containing files newer than last boot, diff against the impermanence bind list (parse /proc/self/mountinfo for @persist/@realm sources — match BOTH, the 2026-07-06 audit initially missed realm-backed binds). This is the detector that would have caught the ~/.claude hole automatically.
2. Kernel-pairing: booted kernel/initrd generation vs /run/current-system; NVIDIA userspace version (current-system path) vs loaded module (/proc/driver/nvidia/version); flag any out-of-tree module mismatch.
3. Live-work inventory: running agent sessions (kitty windows via sinnix-kitty-control list + claude/codex processes + active Workflow/nix builds + borg jobs in flight) -> WARN with names so the operator doesn't reboot mid-work.
4. Pre-wipe archive readiness: .snapshots free space on the pool (rollback snapshot needs headroom; root pool unallocated was 14.97GiB on 07-06 — BLOCK below 10GiB), borg-root-snapshots repo reachable.
5. Pending-wipe preview: top-10 largest paths that the wipe will actually destroy (in @ minus persist/realm binds minus HM-provisioned symlink targets) — the operator sees the blast radius before every reboot.

SWITCH mode:
1. Memory headroom: MemAvailable + PSI memory some avg60 vs thresholds (the 06-24 preflight gate exists per commit 841a2ec — locate it and SUBSUME rather than duplicate: this command becomes the single preflight, the old zram-hygiene/MemAvailable gate folds in or is removed per no-alias rule).
2. Disk: /nix free vs min-free (5G), root unallocated.
3. Flake drift: dirty tree summary, flake.lock inputs that will change vs last build (warn when nixpkgs bump will invalidate the CUDA stack -> until sinnix-jr8 lands, that means a multi-hour build; after, ~15min note).
4. Concurrent-switch guard: another nh/nix build toplevel running (tonight's double-switch pattern).

## Integration
- `switch`/`boot` devshell wrappers call `sinnix-preflight switch` first (WARN prints, BLOCK aborts with override env SINNIX_PREFLIGHT_FORCE=1).
- Reboot: shell alias/wrapper prints the reboot report; never blocks a determined operator (WARN-only + one confirm).
- Drift-sentinel (sinnix-p1n) overlap boundary: p1n is continuous telemetry of config/live divergence; preflight is a point-in-time gate that CONSUMES p1n's latest results if present (do not reimplement sysctl/slice diffing here).

## Verification
- On the current live system (pre-reboot, if still applicable): reboot mode must flag the NVIDIA mismatch, and — before sinnix-6x3 lands — the ~/.claude unpersisted mass.
- Synthetic: create a 200MB dir in home, confirm it appears in the wipe preview; run switch mode during a build, confirm concurrent-switch WARN.

title: Capture-integrity sentinel: cross-source freshness/coverage scoreboard killing the silent-data-loss class
description:
Parent: sinnix-tco (pattern sibling of machine-explain sinnix-6o2). Motivation — THREE independent silent-data-loss incidents found in one night (2026-07-06): (1) network telemetry silently zero for 34 days (wrong NIC name, 2026-04-11..05-15); (2) lynchpin substrate promotion of memory tables silently stalled since 06-30 (15k rows/4.5h in DuckDB vs 447k live); (3) sinnix-borg-drill failed exit 15 on 07-01 with zero surfacing. Same failure class: a capture/pipeline stage dies and nothing notices until an investigation needs the data. The attribution-spec principle 'missing data is missing, never zero' has no enforcement arm.

## Design
One command + product: `lynchpin captures doctor` (CLI + MCP tool + JSON artifact) — a scoreboard over EVERY capture lane, each with: last-record timestamp, expected cadence, current lag, coverage gaps in trailing 30d, and state (fresh/lagging/stalled/gapped). Lanes and their ground-truth probes:
- machine telemetry: telemetry.sqlite max(observed_at) per table (metric_sample 10s, service_state 60s, cgroup tables, gpu, network — per-table, since they fail independently).
- substrate promotion lag: DuckDB max ts vs live SQLite max ts per promoted table (the exact 06-30 stall detector).
- polylogue: polylogued freshness (existing `polylogued status` + archive_coverage/archive_debt MCP outputs — reuse, don't reimplement).
- ActivityWatch, atuin shell, keylog (scribe-tap), asciinema, kitty-scrollback, screenshots, syslog: newest file/row under /realm/data/captures/<lane> vs lane cadence config.
- below store: newest index file age (below microscope must be current when an incident hits).
- backup fleet: last exit status + timestamp per unit (borgbackup-job-persist, root-snapshots, btrbk, check, DRILL — drill failure is a red state, not a warning), via systemctl show ExecMainStatus/ExecMainExitTimestamp or journal.
- sinex: reuse sinex_source_health / sinex_sources_status MCP (do NOT duplicate sinex's own source-health machinery — just surface its top-line into the scoreboard).
Cadence expectations live in a declarative host config (lane -> expected interval + grace), versioned in lynchpin.

## Surfacing
- Exit code nonzero when any lane is stalled/red -> usable in scripts.
- A systemd timer (observability class) writes the JSON artifact to /realm/data/captures/ and emits a desktop notification ONLY on state transitions (fresh->stalled), not repeats.
- machine-explain (sinnix-6o2) §5 substrate-health section consumes this product instead of implementing its own canaries — refactor note for whoever lands second.

## Non-goals
No auto-remediation. No new capture lanes. Not a metrics dashboard — a red/green scoreboard with lag numbers and last-gap dates.

## Verification
- Golden: point at a copy of the 06-30-stalled substrate -> promotion lane reports stalled with correct lag.
- Kill polylogued for 10min in a test window -> lane transitions + single notification.
- borg-drill exit-15 (still unfixed until sinnix-u63) must show red on first live run.

title: Lynchpin 'machine explain': operator-facing narrative view of live machine state, calibrated to this host
description:
Parent: sinnix-tco. Operator vision (2026-07-06): 'run some command and get a clear description of what is going on in the machine, customized for my machine and workloads.' Builds on sinnix-kx4 (ingest/promotion) and sinnix-fjq (vmstat/kill capture) — degrade gracefully with a 'signal unavailable' line where those have not landed yet (never silently omit; attribution-spec: missing data is missing, never zero).

## What exists to build on (verified 2026-07-06)
- lynchpin/core/machine_pressure.py: single-sample threshold classifier (io_psi>0.1, oversleep>50ms, swap>1GB) -> observability_status. Thin; keep as the fast path, subsume into this view.
- lynchpin/analysis/machine/workloads.py: semantic workload classification, daily IO by workload kind, detect_orphans, agent_session_attribution (MCP: machine_workload_summary/machine_orphan_processes/machine_agent_sessions).
- lynchpin/analysis/machine/baselines.py: median/MAD bands + changepoints for io_psi_full, mem_avail_mb, dstate, oversleep.
- process_memory_sample has full smaps_rollup columns (pss_anon_kb etc., 60s? cadence since 06-30); cgroup_memory_sample; service_cgroup_pressure_sample; kill_event (after sinnix-fjq).

## Deliverable
New module lynchpin/analysis/machine/explain.py + CLI `lynchpin machine explain [--window 2h] [--json]` + MCP tool machine_explain. Five sections, each with evidence rows behind every claim:

1. HEADLINE: one sentence + state (ok/elevated/degraded/incident). Not threshold-soup: classified via the SIGNATURE LIBRARY below, e.g. 'Memory: elevated — file-cache refault storm signature (refault +38M/h vs baseline 0.4M/h); no swap cycling; matches incident class cache-thrash (last seen 2026-07-06).'
2. MEMORY ACCOUNTING (the pss_anon doctrine): per-workload-group rollup using pss_anon_kb ONLY (document why: ps RSS overstated kitty 2x via 550MB shared driver/font maps, 2026-07-06 finding). Workload taxonomy = declarative mapping config (lynchpin config file, host-specific, versioned): unit/cgroup/comm patterns -> groups {agents, terminals, mcp-servers, browsers, postgres, sinex-stack, builds, desktop-shell, capture-daemons, other}. Each group: pss_anon now, delta vs 24h ago, delta vs its baseline band, flag if outside band. Include swap/zram occupancy per group once swap exists (swap_kb column present).
3. KNOWN-SIGNATURE DETECTORS — the host's failure-mode library encoded as testable predicates over the window (source: incident timeline 2026-07-06, .agent/scratch/2026-07-06-memory-posture-synthesis.md §2 in sinnix repo):
   - cache-thrash: workingset_refault_file rate >> baseline AND pswpin/out ~0 AND PSI mem some elevated.
   - swap-thrash: sustained pswpin AND pswpout cycling + PSI mem/io full elevated (the 2026-06-07-class signature; post-redesign this is the tripwire that swappiness=10 went wrong -> recommend 5).
   - io-livelock risk: D-state count high + PSI io full high + journal write stall (the 2026-06-03 class).
   - kill-storm: kill_event rate; victim-size histogram (many tiny victims = degenerate victim choice, the 2026-07-04 class).
   - burst-alloc starvation: allocstall rate high while MemAvailable high (the 2026-06-29 class).
   - leak candidates: per-process pss_anon monotone growth fit over 48h (the detector that would have named kitty automatically: 45MB->2.9GB monotone = flag with rate GB/day). Threshold: >200MB/day sustained.
   - writeback pressure: dirty_kb near dirty_bytes limit repeatedly.
   Each detector returns {fires: bool, confidence, evidence: [(ts, metric, value, baseline)], last_seen_incident: date, playbook: str}. Playbook strings reference the known mitigation ('restart offending kitty', 'check earlyoom victims', bead id).
4. CHANGE CONTEXT: boot generation vs current-system generation (staleness), drift-sentinel results (after sinnix-p1n; file under /realm/data/captures/machine/), units started/stopped in window, last switch time, recent kill events. Answers 'what changed?'.
5. SUBSTRATE HEALTH: canaries — telemetry freshness (live sqlite max ts), substrate promotion lag (the silent-stall class from sinnix-kx4), root-disk unallocated GiB, MX500 wear attr, journal size. One line each, only surfaced when outside band.

## Design rules
- All thresholds/bands host-calibrated from baselines.py products, not hardcoded (hardcode only floors where no baseline exists yet, marked TODO-recalibrate).
- Text output tuned for terminal reading: <=40 lines default, every claim followed by its number+baseline in parens; --json returns full evidence for agent consumption.
- The command must run <5s from live SQLite (read-only, no substrate rebuild); heavy joins go against materialized products.
- Reuse machine_pressure.py fields for the fast header; do not duplicate its loader.
- Add `lynchpin machine explain --incident <ts>` variant: same report centered on a past window (post-mortem mode), joining machine_pressure_incidents.json (sinnix-kx4 product).

## Verification
- Golden test: feed the recorded 2026-07-06 00:20-00:24 window (earlyoom killed cc1plus during first switch attempt) -> report must fire kill-storm + name nix-build workload group as the memory driver.
- Leak detector test: feed kitty pid 2724511 history (45MB->2949MB, 07-03..07-05) -> flags leak candidate at ~1GB/day.
- Live smoke: run on the current machine; operator judges usefulness — iterate wording with operator before closing.

title: Config/live drift sentinel: verify live kernel+cgroup state matches evaluated config
description:
Parent: sinnix-tco; synthesis §3c item 5. Motivation: config/live divergence corrupted diagnoses at least 3 times (2026-04-22 slices on stale policy; 2026-05-02; 2026-06-07 — the swappiness=60 cache-hoard era ran KERNEL DEFAULT 60 while the repo had 10 configured since 05-22, so a drift bug was attributed to a tuning value and 'fixed' by escalating to 0/1000). The current 3-week no-reboot window (kernel gen 20260616 vs system gen 20260705, NVIDIA userspace/kernel mismatch breaking new GPU app launches — EGL dri2 failures) is the same class.

## What to build
A sinnix-owned checker (script + oneshot service + timer, observability class) that compares LIVE vs INTENDED and emits structured results:
1. sysctls: every key in boot.kernel.sysctl vs /proc/sys live value.
2. Slice properties: for each slice in the runtime inventory (source of truth: /etc/sinnix/runtime-inventory.json), systemctl show <slice> -p CPUWeight,IOWeight,MemoryHigh,MemoryMax,MemoryLow,ManagedOOMMemoryPressure vs intended.
3. Swap topology: swapon --show vs config (device set, sizes, priorities).
4. Kernel/boot staleness: booted generation vs current-system generation; flag when they diverge (reboot pending) and ESPECIALLY when kernel-module-sensitive packages changed (nvidia driver version file vs loaded module version from /proc/driver/nvidia/version — this exact mismatch broke GPU app launches 2026-07-06).
5. journald effective config, zram presence/size, iocost enable state (/sys/fs/cgroup/io.cost.qos has enable=1 rows), earlyoom cmdline args vs config.
Output: one JSON line per check {check, intended, live, match} to a file under /realm/data/captures/machine/ (so lynchpin can ingest as a source) + non-zero-summary surfaced via sinnix-observe. Do NOT auto-remediate — evidence plane only.

## Verification
Seed a deliberate drift (sysctl -w vm.swappiness=1 transiently), run checker, confirm detection; confirm the current kernel-generation staleness is reported on first run (if still unrebooted).

title: Workload memory baseline: kitty leak, MCP fleet dedup, postgres sizing, sinexd leak handoff
description:
Parent: sinnix-tco; synthesis §3f. The 2026-06-22 audit already concluded 'workload-level direction instead of endlessly tuning cgroups' — this executes it. Standing anon baseline observed 2026-07-06 with browser CLOSED: ~21GB, dominated by:

1. kitty leak: instances at 2.8GB and 1.9GB RSS with scrollback_lines=10000, NO scrollback_pager_history_size, only 3 windows — config is innocent, this is a genuine leak (kitty 0.47.4, long-lived agent TUI sessions with heavy repaint). Actions: (a) immediate: restart the fat kitties (~4GB back); (b) check kitty upstream issues for known 0.47 leaks / try 0.48+; (c) if unresolved upstream, add a recycle practice for agent-host terminals (NOT a cron kill — agents run in them; coordinate with agent-orchestration skill conventions, e.g. recycle on session end); (d) capture a before/after via process_memory_sample to confirm the leak signature (RSS grows without window/scrollback growth).
2. MCP fleet dedup: two serena instances at ~1.2GB each observed; sinnix-mcp-sweep exists (added 2026-06-29) but is manual. Wire it: either a user timer (daily, background slice) or a session-end hook. Verify it only reaps ORPHANED servers (no live agent session attached) — read its source first; the 2026-06-15 lesson (earlyoom killing agents' own MCP servers) means false-positive reaping breaks sessions.
3. Postgres fleet: ~3GB aggregate across sinex postgres + others. Audit shared_buffers/work_mem per instance vs actual DB sizes; sinex postgres (99G DB) likely deserves its allocation, smaller ones may not. Report, then tune only clear waste.
4. sinexd leak: repeated memcg OOMs at 7GB anon (2026-06-18/19/27, kernel log). This is a sinex application bug — file it in the SINEX repo (GitHub issue, self-contained per repo rules: inline the journal evidence, no local paths) and link the issue URL back here. Do not tune the sinnix-side cap as a fix (cap is working as designed: containing the leak).

## Verification
Post-actions baseline: ps_mem-style accounting or process_memory_sample query showing standing anon <=16GB with the same session shape (agents + terminals + MCP, browser closed). Each subsystem's number cited before/after.
notes: KITTY LEAK CONFIRMED EMPIRICALLY 2026-07-06 (telemetry process_memory_sample, live since 06-30): individual kitty PIDs grow monotonically ~45MB anon PSS at spawn -> 2.3-2.9GB over 2-3 days (~1GB/day/instance; e.g. pid 2724511: 45->2949MB over 07-03..07-05; pid 2724383: 46->2310MB over 2 days). Leak is heap-concentrated (single 496MB [heap] region in live smaps; RSS overstates by ~2x due to 550MB shared-clean driver/font maps — use pss_anon_kb for all accounting). Workload correlate: long-lived claude/codex TUI sessions (heavy repaint for days) under kitty 0.47.4 --single-instance. Priority action: try kitty 0.48+ first; check upstream tracker for heap growth with TUI redraw; interim mitigation = recycle agent-host kitties at session end (NOT cron kill). Verification metric: pss_anon_kb of a kitty hosting a 24h agent session stays <300MB.

title: Migrate @sinex subvolume (266G) off the worn MX500 to /realm NVMe
description:
OPERATOR-DECIDED 2026-07-06 (approved: migrate soon). Parent: sinnix-tco. Depends on sinnix-een (headroom + snapshot deletion first). The MX500 root is at ~104% rated NAND wear; @sinex (blob-repository 167G append-only capture blobs + postgresql 99G) is the largest and fastest-growing resident. /realm (CT4000P3SSD8, 22% wear, 400GB/day budget) is the correct home per the established wear policy. sinex postgres is already nodatacow (2026-06 rollout) — preserve that attribute through the move.

## Plan sketch (executor refines)
1. Coordinate with sinex: stop sinexd + postgres (systemctl units per modules/services/ sinex stack), snapshot @sinex read-only.
2. btrfs send the snapshot to /realm (target subvolume e.g. /realm/sinex or fold into /realm data topology — check /realm/project/sinity-lynchpin/docs/reference/realm-map.md for the intended topology and sinex AGENTS.md for path assumptions; SINEX_ROOT env points at the repo, state paths are configured in the sinnix sinex modules).
3. Re-point the sinnix mount: hosts/sinnix-prime storage config mounts /var/lib/sinex from subvol=@sinex on sdc2 — change device to nvme0n1p3 + new subvol. Keep the /var/lib/sinex mountpoint so sinex config is untouched (path stability = smallest blast radius).
4. Verify nodatacow (lsattr on postgresql dir), start stack, run sinex health checks (xtask or sinex_system_health MCP), watch sinex_source_health for capture continuity.
5. After 48h burn-in: delete old @sinex from sdc2, run filtered balance again (frees ~266G — root drops to ~40% and unallocated recovers massively).
6. Update wear tracking: the standing sinex write load (~1GB/hr post-nodatacow) moves to the NVMe budget; note in lynchpin observability_status wear SLO tracking.

## Risks
- btrfs send of active-write subvolume: requires the stack stopped (capture gap — schedule + note in sinex source continuity; polylogued/scribe-tap buffering behavior should be checked so the gap is clean, not corrupt).
- Impermanence interactions: /var/lib/sinex is a direct subvolume mount, not persist-bind — confirm in storage.nix before assuming.

## Verification
Full sinex pipeline green post-move (sources status, event flow, MCP tools respond), zero data loss across the move window (compare event counts/blob counts before/after), root disk usage delta ≈ -266G.

title: Config streamlining: remove cargo-cult/redundant/contradictory resource settings
description:
Parent: sinnix-tco. From the 2026-07-06 resource-control audit (full inventory in session; key items here are self-contained). Per repo No-Alias Rule: full removal in one pass, no compatibility shims. Items NOT already covered by sinnix-mys/sinnix-3gb:

1. transmission.nix:134-141 — serviceConfig override duplicates its background-maintenance resource class byte-for-byte (Nice=10, CPUWeight=5, IO idle, IOWeight=5, MemoryHigh=1G, MemoryMax=3G vs runtime-defaults.nix:71-78). Delete the override; class inheritance covers it. Verify: systemctl show transmission -p MemoryHigh unchanged after switch.
2. performance.nix:312-315 — nix.slice parent wrapper sets CPUWeight=5/IOWeight=2 identical to its only child nix-build.slice. Fold: remove the parent-slice resource block (keep the slice decl only if systemd hierarchy requires it for the dash-naming; check whether nh/nix-daemon units reference nix.slice directly).
3. .agent/scratch/2026-05-07-agent-oomd-scope.md — describes ManagedOOM policy on background.slice + sinnix-oomd-watch, both since reverted (zero grep hits in tree). Move to .agent/scratch/archive/ with a one-line header noting it documents a reverted era (it misleads agents doing archaeology; the agent.slice half DID land and is current).
4. CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 contradiction: runtime-defaults.nix:202 (build command class) disables incremental compilation while build-policy.nix:96-101 justifies not using sccache BECAUSE local Rust relies on incremental warm rebuilds. Resolve: remove CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 from the build class (incremental stays on, matching the sccache rationale) UNLESS git/scratch archaeology shows a measured reason it was added — if found, document it inline instead and reconcile the sccache comment.
5. /realm mount options: relatime,lazytime while root is noatime (hosts/sinnix-prime storage config). Unify on noatime+lazytime for /realm (capture lake = write-heavy, atime writes are pure waste). One-line fstab/storage.nix change.
6. Journald: Storage=persistent + SystemMaxUse=32G currently writes to the worn-out MX500 (8.6G resident; drive at ~104% rated NAND endurance, see sinnix-dsk issue). The 2026-05-22 volatile decision was reverted (journal is now the kill-event forensic source — keep persistent) but cap SystemMaxUse=4G and consider Compress=yes. 4G still holds ~weeks at observed volume. File location: wherever services.journald is configured (grep modules/ for journald; may be NixOS default currently = implicit).
7. Comment-drift fixes not covered elsewhere: docs/prime-bios-settings.md wrongly frames the old Gen2 PCIe cap as intentional (operator-confirmed accidental, memory project_pcie_gen2_bios_fix) — correct it or delete the framing.

## Verification
Single switch; for each item cite systemctl show / findmnt / journalctl -b output proving behavior unchanged where intended and changed where intended. No standalone nix eval probes (repo rule).
notes: 2026-07-06: 6/7 items done. (1) transmission.nix mkRuntimeServiceConfig 'overrides' block removed — confirmed byte-identical to background-maintenance class defaults already applied via resourceClass, verified against runtime-defaults.nix. (2) performance.nix nix.slice parent sliceConfig removed entirely — confirmed byte-identical CPUWeight/IOWeight to its only child nix-build.slice; systemd creates nix.slice implicitly via dash-hierarchy, no unit needs it explicitly declared (verified nothing else references nix.slice except a telemetry cgroup-path string, which works against an implicit slice fine). (3) 2026-05-07-agent-oomd-scope.md moved to archive/ with a header noting it's a reverted era (agent.slice DID land; ManagedOOM policy + sinnix-oomd-watch did not — zero grep hits confirmed). (4) CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 removed from build envDefaults — git archaeology found no measured rationale (added mechanically alongside an unrelated job-count bump), so resolved per the bead's default: incremental stays on, matching the sccache-disabled rationale already documented in build-policy.nix. (5) /realm mount: relatime -> noatime (now matches root). (6) journald SystemMaxUse 32G -> 4G on hosts/sinnix-prime/default.nix (the actually-live mkForce override; modules/diagnostics.nix's 50G block is fully shadowed there but left untouched — it may be live for sinnix-ethereal/sinnix-gw, out of scope). Compress=yes was already set. Also inline-documented the persistent-vs-volatile reversal as deliberate (satisfies u63 item 6 too). (7) docs/prime-bios-settings.md: does NOT EXIST anywhere in the tree (checked full repo, not just docs/) — bead's premise unverifiable/stale, no action taken. nix eval of full nixosConfigurations.sinnix-prime.config.system.build.toplevel.drvPath succeeds with these changes. NOT YET switch-verified.
close_reason: 6/7 items landed and verified: transmission.nix duplicate override removed (live values unchanged, confirmed via systemctl show); nix.slice redundant CPUWeight/IOWeight removed (nix-build.slice values unaffected); reverted-era scratch doc archived; CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 removed (no measured rationale found in git history); /realm noatime live; journald SystemMaxUse=4G Compress=yes live. Item 7 (docs/prime-bios-settings.md comment fix): file does not exist anywhere in the repo — verified via full-tree search, bead's premise unverifiable/stale, no action possible. Commit fa8e217.

title: Build pipeline: max-jobs 1/cores 16, cudaCapabilities 8.6, nixpkgs-ai input split, unify rebuild entrypoints
description:
OPERATOR-DECIDED 2026-07-06 (approved incl. nixpkgs-ai manual-only bump cadence). Parent: sinnix-tco; synthesis §3d. Independent of the memory-posture issues; can land anytime.

## Context
2026-07-06 switch took ~2h: flake.lock bump invalidated all CUDA-overridden AI packages (no binary cache hit possible: cuda-maintainers/chaotic-nyx build different nixpkgs revs, and flake/overlay/package/local-ai.nix does app-level overrides those caches don't build). Each ggml build compiles kernels for NINE architectures (sm_75..sm_121a) while the host has exactly one GPU: RTX 3080 = compute capability 8.6. Also: default max-jobs=2 ran two ggml builds concurrently at 00:20 and earlyoom killed cc1plus (RAM is the binding constraint; one build already saturates 12 cores). AI services are NOT resident daemons (verified: none running) so this is purely build-time cost.

## Exact changes
1. modules/build-policy.nix:57-58: max-jobs 2 -> 1, cores 12 -> 16.
   flake/command-registry.nix:78-81 + flake/dev-shell.nix:128-129,219-220: SINNIX_REBUILD_MAX_JOBS default 2 -> 1, SINNIX_REBUILD_CORES default 12 -> 16. Keep env override capability.
   runtime-defaults.nix build command class env (CARGO_BUILD_JOBS/CMAKE/MAKEFLAGS/NIX_BUILD_CORES=12): bump to 16 for consistency or leave — executor judgment, note the choice.
2. CUDA arch narrowing: set cudaCapabilities=["8.6"] for the AI package set. Implementation: in flake/overlay/package/local-ai.nix, import/override the cuda package scope with config.cudaCapabilities=["8.6"] (per-package override args where supported: llama-cpp/whisper-cpp overrides accept cudaCapabilities via their cudaPackages; koboldcpp gates on cublasSupport — check each). Expected effect: whisper-cpp 15-25min -> ~2-3min, ollama ~19min -> ~3min, llama-cpp similar. Verify by timing one rebuild.
3. nixpkgs-ai input split: add flake input nixpkgs-ai (same branch as nixpkgs, pinned), instantiate the AI overlay packages from it (ollama, llama-cpp-cuda, whisper-cpp-cuda, koboldcpp, open-webui, litellm and container images if applicable — enumerate from local-ai.nix + modules/services/). Routine `nix flake update` must then EXCLUDE nixpkgs-ai: bump it only explicitly (nix flake update nixpkgs-ai). Document in the update devshell command / CLAUDE.md includes.
4. Unify rebuild entrypoints: command-registry.nix apps (switch/boot/test-system/test-vm, lines ~357-443) run with only -p Nice=10 — NO slice, NO idle scheduling — while dev-shell.nix mkNhCommand uses --slice=nix-build.slice + CPUSchedulingPolicy=idle + IOSchedulingClass=idle. Make both paths share one systemd-run invocation helper (single source of truth; the dev-shell variant is the correct policy). This closes the escape hatch where `nix run .#switch` bypasses all build containment.
5. Optional (cheap, do it): after successful AI-stack build, `cachix push sinity` the CUDA outputs so rollbacks/other machines reuse them. sinity.cachix.org is already a substituter with key configured.

## Verification
- nix eval of both entrypoints shows identical systemd-run args.
- Time `switch` after touching nixpkgs-ai: AI stack rebuild <=25min total.
- Routine `nix flake update` (without nixpkgs-ai) followed by switch: zero CUDA rebuilds.
notes: 2026-07-06: code-complete. cudaCapabilities=[8.6] via nixpkgs pkgsForCudaArch.sm_86 (confirmed correct nixpkgs API via docs — local-ai.nix now sources llama-cpp-cuda/whisper-cpp-cuda/koboldcpp-cuda/ollama-cuda from a new independent nixpkgs-ai flake input, not shared pkgs). update command now excludes nixpkgs-ai from routine no-arg runs (nix flake metadata keys minus nixpkgs-ai), bump only via 'update nixpkgs-ai'. Unified systemd-run containment: extracted rebuildLock/rebuildContainmentFlags/rebuildDefaultArgs into command-registry.nix, both 'nix run .#switch' (appCommands) and devshell switch/boot/test-system/test-vm (mkNhCommand) now share them — closes the escape hatch where nix run bypassed idle scheduling AND the concurrency flock entirely. cachix push sinity: deferred to post-switch (needs actual built outputs). Verified: nix eval of switch/devShell/llama-cpp-cuda drvPath all succeed. NOT YET switch-verified (needs Step-1 batch switch + timed rebuild).
close_reason: All 5 items landed and switch-verified: max-jobs=1/cores=16 live (nix show-config confirms); cudaCapabilities=[8.6] via pkgsForCudaArch.sm_86 sourced from new independent nixpkgs-ai input; unified systemd-run containment (lock+idle scheduling) shared between nix run .#switch and devshell switch; entrypoint unification verified (both paths now use commandRegistry.rebuildLock/rebuildContainmentFlags). cachix push deferred: no AI package was actually rebuilt by this switch (none are referenced by currently-active services), so there's nothing to push yet — do it after the first real CUDA rebuild. Commit fa8e217.
